Tinus ovata, a species of tree in the genus Tinus, is a type of plant commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The leaves of Tinus ovata have been used in traditional medicine for their health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, potentially aiding in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and skin diseases. The bioactive constituents of Tinus ovata leaves include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to their medicinal properties. The leaves of Tinus ovata are commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce inflammation, and support overall well-being.
